faq.title: Oven & Stove Repair – Frequently Asked Questions

items.a: If your food takes too long to cook or stays undercooked, the issue is often a burned-out heating element (electric ovens), a faulty igniter (gas ovens), or a defective thermostat or temperature sensor. Don't wait — this problem usually gets worse over time.

If a burner won't turn on or heats unevenly, possible causes include a damaged burner coil or element, clogged gas ports, or a faulty ignition switch or spark module. Tip: Uneven heat can ruin cookware and food quality.

If your oven is completely unresponsive, common causes include a tripped circuit breaker, a faulty control board, or wiring/power supply issues. Quick fix: Check your breaker first — if that's not it, you likely need service.
If your food burns or remains undercooked, this may be due to a faulty temperature sensor, calibration issues, or a failing thermostat. Warning: This can lead to consistently wasted food.
If you notice a gas smell or delayed ignition, causes may include a weak or dirty igniter, gas valve issues, or blocked burner ports. Safety Alert: This is dangerous — call for service immediately.

items.q: Control Panel or Knobs Not Responding
items.a: If you can't adjust temperature or turn on your cooktop, the issue may be worn knobs, a failed control board, or moisture/grease damage. Common in older units or after spills.

If your oven won't lock, unlock, or start cleaning, it may be due to a faulty door latch, control board issues, or overheating protection. Important: Avoid forcing the door — it can cause more damage.
If heat escapes or cooking is uneven, common causes include a worn-out door seal, broken hinges, or a misaligned door. This increases energy bills and cooking time.
